---
title: "Scientific Computing I: Foundations of Numerical Methods"
subtitle: "Core Mathematics — Year 1"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Scientific Computing I: Foundations of Numerical Methods

**Track**: Core Mathematics — Year 1

Practical and theoretical foundations of numerical computation. Every model in the curriculum eventually requires numerical implementation; this course teaches the student to do it correctly, efficiently, and with rigorous error control.

## Table of Contents

1. Floating-Point Arithmetic and Error Analysis
2. Root Finding
3. Polynomial Interpolation and Approximation
4. Numerical Differentiation and Integration
5. Direct Methods for Linear Systems
6. Iterative Methods for Linear Systems
7. Eigenvalue Problems
8. Numerical Methods for Initial Value ODEs
9. Fast Algorithms: FFT, Fast Multipole, and Randomized Methods
10. Software Engineering for Scientific Computing

---

## Chapter 1: Floating-Point Arithmetic and Error Analysis

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Root Finding

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Polynomial Interpolation and Approximation

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Numerical Differentiation and Integration

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Direct Methods for Linear Systems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Iterative Methods for Linear Systems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Eigenvalue Problems

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Numerical Methods for Initial Value ODEs

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 9: Fast Algorithms: FFT, Fast Multipole, and Randomized Methods

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 10: Software Engineering for Scientific Computing

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
